title: CSRF on Bulk Order Export — No Nonce for Non-Tab AJAX Route
vulnerability_description: The ajax_gate() method in class-wc-order-export-admin.php routes AJAX requests based on whether a 'tab' parameter matches a registered tab key. For requests WITHOUT a valid tab parameter, the request is forwarded to WC_Order_Export_Ajax without any nonce verification. The ajax_export_download_bulk_file method within WC_Order_Export_Ajax has no nonce check of its own. Only a capability check (view_woocommerce_reports or export_woocommerce_orders) is enforced. An attacker who can trick a logged-in WooCommerce manager/admin into visiting a malicious page can trigger a full order export via cross-site request forgery.

affected_component.description: No nonce check before calling WC_Order_Export_Ajax methods when tab is empty/invalid

code_evidence.vulnerable_path: if ( ! isset( $this->tabs[ $tab ] ) ) { $ajax_handler = apply_filters(..., new WC_Order_Export_Ajax()); if ( ! method_exists( $ajax_handler, $method ) ) { die(...); } $ajax_handler->$method(); die(); }
code_evidence.nonce_check_location: Only on line 546: if (! check_admin_referer('woe_nonce', 'woe_nonce')), which is ONLY reached when $tab IS a valid registered tab — this is the else branch.
code_evidence.contrast_with_ajax_run_one_job: ajax_run_one_job (line 17) calls check_ajax_referer('woe_nonce', 'woe_nonce') — correctly protected. ajax_export_download_bulk_file has no such check.

attack_scenario.description: Attacker creates a page with an img tag or auto-submitting form that sends a GET/POST to /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php?action=order_exporter&method=export_download_bulk_file&export_bulk_profile=now. When a logged-in WooCommerce manager visits the attacker's page, the browser sends the request with the victim's cookies. The server exports all orders matching the 'Export Now' profile.
attack_scenario.impact_browser_download: The exported file is sent as a file download to the victim's browser — the attacker does not directly receive the file. Impact is indirect (forcing export, potential UI confusion).
attack_scenario.impact_with_pro_destinations: If the export profile is configured to send to FTP/email/HTTP endpoint (Pro feature), CSRF could trigger exfiltration of order data to an attacker-controlled server — but that requires the profile to be pre-configured with an attacker destination, which requires prior access.
attack_scenario.realistic_impact: Low in free tier (file goes to victim's browser). Medium concern in enterprise deployments with remote export destinations.

poc.notes: No nonce parameter needed. The request triggers a full WooCommerce order export when made by an authenticated user with view_woocommerce_reports capability.

remediation.recommendation: Add a nonce check at the top of the no-tab branch in ajax_gate(), OR add an explicit nonce check at the top of WC_Order_Export_Ajax::ajax_export_download_bulk_file(). The check should call check_ajax_referer('woe_nonce', 'woe_nonce') and die on failure.
